scenario: Nimbus Payments, a fintech processing 75000 transactions per hour, configures a Camunda 8.8 REST outbound connector to call a partner gateway. A developer must reference the secret PARTNER_API_KEY in the Authorization header of a FEEL context map without exposing the value anywhere in the BPMN model or in process variables.
question: Which Authorization header value uses the documented secrets syntax correctly inside the FEEL expression?

options.text: Set Authorization to secrets.PARTNER_API_KEY directly inside the FEEL map without quotes, treating the secret like a regular process variable that the engine resolves at runtime
Set Authorization to {{ secrets.PARTNER_API_KEY }} wrapped in backticks inside the FEEL map so the engine treats it as a template literal rather than a plain string value
Set Authorization to ${secrets.PARTNER_API_KEY} inside the FEEL map so the dollar-brace template is expanded by the connector at execution time as a configured secret reference
Set Authorization to "{{secrets.PARTNER_API_KEY}}" wrapped in double quotes inside the FEEL map so the placeholder is passed through and resolved by the connector at execution time

a.text: Incorrect. Secrets are not variables, and the bare secrets.* form is the deprecated syntax; the documented placeholder is {{secrets.NAME}} and it must be wrapped in double quotes inside a FEEL expression.

b.text: Incorrect. FEEL has no backtick template-literal syntax; the documented form is {{secrets.NAME}} inside a regular double-quoted string within the FEEL expression.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented placeholder is {{secrets.NAME}}, not the dollar-brace form; the connector replaces the {{...}} placeholder at execution time, not a shell-style template.

d.text: Correct. The placeholder {{secrets.PARTNER_API_KEY}} is wrapped in double quotes inside the FEEL expression; the engine passes the placeholder through to the connector, which then resolves the secret on execution.

explanation: Secrets are referenced with the {{secrets.NAME}} placeholder. Because secrets are not variables, the placeholder must be wrapped in double quotes when used in a FEEL expression. The engine passes the placeholder through and the connector resolves it at execution time.

docs.title: How to use connectors - using secrets
